 {"id":"bd-35ld","title":"WebSocket E2E test suite: protocol conformance and cancel-correctness","description":"# WebSocket E2E Test Suite

## Overview
Comprehensive end-to-end test suite for WebSocket implementation covering
protocol conformance, cancel-correctness, and real-world usage patterns.

## Test Directory Structure
```
tests/e2e/websocket/
├── mod.rs                      # Test module root
├── conformance/
│   ├── handshake.rs            # Upgrade handshake tests
│   ├── framing.rs              # Frame format tests
│   ├── close.rs                # Close handshake tests
│   ├── ping_pong.rs            # Keepalive tests
│   └── fragmentation.rs        # Fragmented message tests
├── cancel_correctness/
│   ├── client_cancel.rs        # Client Cx cancellation
│   ├── server_cancel.rs        # Server Cx cancellation
│   ├── region_close.rs         # Region-scoped cleanup
│   └── mid_message_cancel.rs   # Cancel during message
├── integration/
│   ├── echo_server.rs          # Basic echo tests
│   ├── chat_room.rs            # Multi-client broadcast
│   ├── binary_stream.rs        # Large binary transfer
│   └── reconnection.rs         # Connection recovery
└── lab/
    ├── deterministic.rs        # Lab runtime tests
    └── schedule_exploration.rs # DPOR schedule testing
```

## Test Categories

### 1. Conformance Tests (RFC 6455)
- Handshake validation (headers, key computation)
- Frame format (opcode, length, masking)
- Close codes and reasons
- Ping/pong timing
- Message fragmentation/reassembly

### 2. Cancel-Correctness Tests
- Client Cx cancelled mid-send
- Client Cx cancelled mid-recv
- Server Cx cancelled during accept
- Region close with active connections
- Graceful shutdown under load

### 3. Integration Tests
- Echo server round-trip
- Multi-client chat broadcast
- Binary file transfer (large messages)
- Reconnection after network failure

### 4. Lab Runtime Tests
- Deterministic replay of WebSocket interactions
- Schedule exploration for race conditions
- Virtual time for timeout testing

## Logging Requirements

### Log Levels
- TRACE: Frame-level details (opcode, length, first 64 bytes)
- DEBUG: Message-level (send/recv, size)
- INFO: Connection lifecycle (connect, close, error)
- WARN: Protocol anomalies (unexpected frames)
- ERROR: Failures with context

### Structured Log Fields
```rust
tracing::info\!(
    test_name = %test_name,
    ws_state = ?state,
    message_count = count,
    bytes_transferred = bytes,
    close_code = ?code,
    duration_ms = elapsed.as_millis(),
    "WebSocket test completed"
);
```

### Artifact Collection
- Per-test log file
- Trace event timeline
- Frame dump on failure
- Network capture (optional)

## Test Scripts

### Run All WebSocket Tests
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

export RUST_LOG="asupersync=debug,test=info"
export RUST_BACKTRACE=1

echo "=== WebSocket E2E Tests ==="
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_websocket -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture 2>&1 | tee websocket_tests.log

echo "=== Results ==="
grep -E '(PASS|FAIL|test result)' websocket_tests.log
```

### Run Conformance Only
```bash
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_websocket conformance:: -- --nocapture
```

### Run with Lab Runtime
```bash
cargo test -p asupersync --test e2e_websocket lab:: -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All conformance tests pass
- [ ] Cancel-correctness verified for all scenarios
- [ ] Integration tests with real TCP connections
- [ ] Lab runtime tests for deterministic replay
- [ ] Structured logging with artifact collection
- [ ] Test scripts documented and working
- [ ] CI integration (run on every PR)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:19:00.261842694Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T11:28:55.563296681Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T11:28:55.563219558Z","close_reason":"Implemented WebSocket E2E suite: RFC6455 conformance (handshake/framing/fragmentation/ping-pong/close), cancel-correctness (client send cancel + server accept cancel), real TCP integration (echo, 1MiB binary stream, reconnection), and deterministic lab tests (replay + multi-seed smoke). Added runner script scripts/test_websocket_e2e.sh with per-run log capture under target/e2e-results. Fixed RFC6455 masking enforcement in FrameCodec (client->server must be masked; server->client must be unmasked).","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","ecosystem-parity","websocket"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35ld","depends_on_id":"bd-18mg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35ld","depends_on_id":"bd-1bmj","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35ld","depends_on_id":"bd-3hn8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35ld","depends_on_id":"bd-ku6n","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35ld","depends_on_id":"bd-rww6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:10Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":107,"issue_id":"bd-35ld","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Claimed 2026-02-01: implementing WebSocket E2E suite (RFC6455 conformance + cancel-correctness) under tests/e2e/websocket/. Will add deterministic lab tests + real TCP integration tests + runner script(s) with structured logs/artifacts. Target: no flaky timing, one-thread test execution, rich tracing fields (conn_id, opcode, len, close_code, cancel_reason).","created_at":"2026-02-01T09:06:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35lp","title":"[ALIEN][RAPTORQ][PERF] Safe SIMD XOR acceleration (std::simd)","description":"## Purpose

- Must be safe (no unsafe blocks).
- Must preserve determinism.

## Acceptance criteria

- One SIMD lever produces measurable improvement.
- Fallback path exists for platforms without required SIMD.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:16:22.041612745Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T10:50:19.113384363Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T10:50:19.113296360Z","close_reason":"Safe SIMD XOR acceleration implemented: (1) gf256_add_slice uses u64 wide-XOR via chunks_exact_mut(8), (2) gf256_mul_slice uses pre-built 256-byte table for inputs >= 256 bytes, (3) gf256_addmul_slice refactored to share build_mul_table helper. All safe (no unsafe), all 22 GF256 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien","performance","raptorq"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35lp","depends_on_id":"bd-1kdw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T01:54:23Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3l4f","title":"bd-ut19: security symbol authentication tests","description":"## Unit Tests for src/security/ (850 LOC, 26 existing tests)

Replace misleading auth bead. Security module implements SYMBOL AUTHENTICATION (keyed hashing for RaptorQ symbols), not credential/token auth.

### New Test Cases — key.rs (150 LOC, 8 tests)
- Key derivation: different seeds produce different keys
- Key derivation: same seed always produces same key (deterministic)
- Key uniqueness: 1000 random seeds, no collisions
- Key import/export: round-trip through bytes

### New Test Cases — context.rs (227 LOC, 6 tests)
- Sign + verify round-trip: sign symbol → verify with same key → Ok
- Cross-key verification: sign with key A, verify with key B → Err
- Empty symbol signing: zero-length payload
- Maximum-size symbol signing: near max payload
- Verify with tampered tag (bit flip) → rejection

### New Test Cases — authenticated.rs (119 LOC, 3 tests)
- AuthenticatedSymbol verified=true for locally created
- AuthenticatedSymbol verified=false for received-from-network
- into_symbol strips authentication metadata
- Verify then use: verify() → take inner symbol

### New Test Cases — tag.rs (178 LOC, 6 tests)
- Tag equality: same input → same tag
- Tag inequality: different input → different tag
- Tag Display/Debug formatting
- Tag serialization round-trip

### Logging Requirements
Each test logs: key seed, symbol size, tag value, verification result.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 15+ new tests across security submodules
- [ ] Sign/verify round-trip exhaustively tested
- [ ] Tampering detection verified
- [ ] All AuthMode variants exercised","status":"tombstone","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T20:16:07.827898910Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.398173936Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T20:24:15.311572325Z","close_reason":"26 existing tests already cover security module","deleted_at":"2026-02-03T00:03:40.398160922Z","deleted_by":"ubuntu","delete_reason":"delete","original_type":"task","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["security","unit-test"]}
 {"id":"bd-3lbt","title":"Async directory operations: readdir, mkdir, remove with io_uring","description":"# Async Directory Operations

## Overview
Implements async directory operations using io_uring where possible, with fallback
to blocking pool for operations not supported by io_uring.

## Design

### Directory Operations
```rust
/// Async filesystem operations.
pub mod fs {
    /// Create directory.
    pub async fn create_dir(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
        {
            // IORING_OP_MKDIRAT
            let sqe = opcode::MkdirAt::new(
                types::Fd(libc::AT_FDCWD),
                path.as_os_str().as_bytes().as_ptr() as *const _,
            )
            .mode(0o755);
            
            let cqe = uring.submit_and_wait(sqe).await?;
            cqe.result()?;
            Ok(())
        }
        
        #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
        {
            blocking_pool::spawn(cx, || std::fs::create_dir(path)).await
        }
    }
    
    /// Create directory and all parents.
    pub async fn create_dir_all(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError>;
    
    /// Remove file.
    pub async fn remove_file(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
        {
            // IORING_OP_UNLINKAT
            let sqe = opcode::UnlinkAt::new(
                types::Fd(libc::AT_FDCWD),
                path.as_os_str().as_bytes().as_ptr() as *const _,
            );
            
            let cqe = uring.submit_and_wait(sqe).await?;
            cqe.result()?;
            Ok(())
        }
        
        #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
        blocking_pool::spawn(cx, || std::fs::remove_file(path)).await
    }
    
    /// Remove empty directory.
    pub async fn remove_dir(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError>;
    
    /// Remove directory and contents recursively.
    pub async fn remove_dir_all(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError>;
    
    /// Rename file or directory.
    pub async fn rename(cx: &Cx, from: &Path, to: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError> {
        cx.checkpoint()?;
        
        #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
        {
            // IORING_OP_RENAMEAT
            let sqe = opcode::RenameAt::new(
                types::Fd(libc::AT_FDCWD),
                from.as_os_str().as_bytes().as_ptr() as *const _,
                types::Fd(libc::AT_FDCWD),
                to.as_os_str().as_bytes().as_ptr() as *const _,
            );
            
            let cqe = uring.submit_and_wait(sqe).await?;
            cqe.result()?;
            Ok(())
        }
        
        #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
        blocking_pool::spawn(cx, || std::fs::rename(from, to)).await
    }
    
    /// Read metadata.
    pub async fn metadata(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<Metadata, IoError>;
    
    /// Create symbolic link.
    pub async fn symlink(cx: &Cx, original: &Path, link: &Path) -> Outcome<(), IoError>;
    
    /// Read symbolic link target.
    pub async fn read_link(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<PathBuf, IoError>;
    
    /// Copy file.
    pub async fn copy(cx: &Cx, from: &Path, to: &Path) -> Outcome<u64, IoError>;
}
```

### ReadDir
```rust
/// Async directory reader.
pub struct ReadDir {
    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    uring_reader: UringReadDir,
    
    #[cfg(not(target_os = "linux"))]
    blocking_reader: BlockingReadDir,
}

impl ReadDir {
    /// Open directory for reading.
    pub async fn open(cx: &Cx, path: &Path) -> Outcome<Self, IoError>;
    
    /// Read next entry.
    pub async fn next_entry(&mut self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Option<DirEntry>, IoError>;
}

pub struct DirEntry {
    pub path: PathBuf,
    pub file_name: OsString,
    pub file_type: FileType,
}

impl DirEntry {
    /// Get metadata for entry.
    pub async fn metadata(&self, cx: &Cx) -> Outcome<Metadata, IoError>;
}
```

### io_uring Operations Used
- IORING_OP_MKDIRAT: Create directory
- IORING_OP_UNLINKAT: Remove file/directory
- IORING_OP_RENAMEAT: Rename
- IORING_OP_STATX: Get metadata
- IORING_OP_SYMLINKAT: Create symlink
- IORING_OP_LINKAT: Create hard link

## Dependencies
- Requires: io_uring File integration (bd-3vb8)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] create_dir/create_dir_all
- [ ] remove_file/remove_dir/remove_dir_all
- [ ] rename
- [ ] ReadDir iteration
- [ ] metadata
- [ ] symlink/read_link
- [ ] copy
- [ ] Fallback on non-Linux
- [ ] Unit tests
- [ ] Integration tests","notes":"## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
- `fs::tests::create_dir_new` - Create single directory
- `fs::tests::create_dir_all_nested` - Create nested directories
- `fs::tests::remove_file_exists` - Remove existing file
- `fs::tests::remove_file_not_found` - Handle missing file error
- `fs::tests::remove_dir_empty` - Remove empty directory
- `fs::tests::remove_dir_all_recursive` - Remove directory tree
- `fs::tests::rename_file` - Rename/move file
- `fs::tests::rename_directory` - Rename directory
- `fs::tests::metadata_file` - Get file metadata
- `fs::tests::metadata_dir` - Get directory metadata
- `fs::tests::symlink_create` - Create symbolic link
- `fs::tests::read_link` - Read symlink target
- `fs::tests::copy_file` - Copy file contents

### ReadDir Tests
- `fs::readdir::iterate_entries` - Iterate directory entries
- `fs::readdir::entry_metadata` - Get metadata per entry
- `fs::readdir::entry_file_type` - File type detection
- `fs::readdir::empty_directory` - Handle empty dir
- `fs::readdir::large_directory` - Handle 10k+ entries

### Cancel-Correctness Tests
- `fs::cancel::cancel_during_create_dir_all` - Cancel nested mkdir
- `fs::cancel::cancel_during_remove_dir_all` - Cancel recursive remove
- `fs::cancel::cancel_during_copy` - Cancel mid-copy
- `fs::cancel::cancel_readdir_iteration` - Cancel directory iteration

### Platform Tests
- `fs::platform::linux_io_uring` - Verify io_uring path on Linux
- `fs::platform::fallback_blocking_pool` - Verify fallback on non-Linux

### Integration Tests
- `fs::integration::create_remove_cycle` - Full lifecycle
- `fs::integration::concurrent_operations` - Multiple parallel ops
- `fs::integration::permission_handling` - Permission errors
- `fs::integration::unicode_paths` - Unicode in file names

### Logging Requirements
- TRACE: Individual syscall/SQE details
- DEBUG: Directory operation start/end
- INFO: Recursive operation progress
- WARN: Permission denied, partial operations
- ERROR: Failures with full path context

### Test Script
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
export RUST_LOG="asupersync::io::fs=debug,test=info"
cargo test -p asupersync fs:: -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee fs_tests.log
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"QuietCat","created_at":"2026-02-01T01:29:23.660424063Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T05:42:19.388930567Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T05:42:19.388831453Z","close_reason":"Added io_uring opcodes (MkDirAt, UnlinkAt+AT_REMOVEDIR, RenameAt, SymlinkAt) to dir.rs and path_ops.rs. All 8 tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ecosystem-parity","filesystem","linux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lbt","depends_on_id":"bd-1qob","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3lbt","depends_on_id":"bd-3vb8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-3m6d","title":"[Conformance] Implement Channel Test Suite","description":"## Overview

Implement the Channel conformance test suite covering MPSC, oneshot, broadcast, and watch channels.

## Current Status

**BLOCKED** - This task depends on asupersync-ocj3 (Core Testing Framework) which provides the `conformance_test!` macro and `ConformanceRuntime` trait.

### Analysis (by AmberCrest):
- **CHAN-001 through CHAN-005, CHAN-007, CHAN-008**: These test cases are already implemented as standard unit tests in the channel modules (src/channel/mpsc.rs, src/channel/oneshot.rs, src/channel/watch.rs)
- **CHAN-006 (Broadcast)**: Cannot be implemented - no broadcast channel exists in the codebase. Requires implementing a broadcast channel first.

### Existing Coverage:
| Test | Status | Location |
|------|--------|----------|
| CHAN-001 (MPSC Ordering) | ✓ Covered | mpsc.rs: fifo_ordering_single_sender, fifo_ordering |
| CHAN-002 (Multi-Producer) | ✓ Covered | mpsc.rs: multi_producer_all_messages_received |
| CHAN-003 (Backpressure) | ✓ Covered | mpsc.rs: backpressure_blocks_until_recv |
| CHAN-004 (Oneshot Success) | ✓ Covered | oneshot.rs: basic_send_recv, reserve_then_send |
| CHAN-005 (Oneshot Sender Dropped) | ✓ Covered | oneshot.rs: sender_dropped_without_send |
| CHAN-006 (Broadcast) | ✗ Missing | No broadcast channel implementation |
| CHAN-007 (Watch Latest Value) | ✓ Covered | watch.rs: latest_value_wins |
| CHAN-008 (Channel Closed) | ✓ Covered | mpsc.rs: recv_after_sender_dropped_drains_queue |

### Remaining Work:
1. Wait for asupersync-ocj3 (Core Testing Framework) to be implemented

 {"id":"bd-3mqxu","title":"[SPORK][App][Crash] Crashpack Hook: emit minimal repro on failure","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:43:30.905076970Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T05:45:20.401040289Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["app","crash","replay","spork"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3mqxu","depends_on_id":"bd-1zkki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mqxu","depends_on_id":"bd-2md12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mqxu","depends_on_id":"bd-qbcnu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:13Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-3mqxu","depends_on_id":"bd-sd083","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:13Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":280,"issue_id":"bd-3mqxu","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Goal

When an app run fails (panic, invariant violation, etc.), produce the minimal deterministic artifacts required to reproduce.

This task is specifically about *hooking* the existing crashpack pipeline into the application lifecycle.

## Deliverable

- Define where crashpack building is initiated:
  - app root
  - supervisor decision points
  - trace recorder

- Ensure crashpack contents are deterministic:
  - stable ordering
  - canonical trace fingerprint

## Acceptance

- Failing app scenario produces a crashpack manifest and minimal divergent prefix.
- Replay uses crashpack to deterministically reproduce the failure.
","created_at":"2026-02-06T05:45:20Z"}]}
